$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 12 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: While the paddock chose sides in the glasshouse war, the yellow-and-red garage declined to notice there was one. The MADONNA 456's winter work was refinement rather than revolution, a brake-stability evolution and a PALM 190 V10 mapping package polished to surgical standard, and Senna turned both into the usual arithmetic. Asselin kept the door with the same gloved calm. The house watched Iris and Azalea spend a season's energy on each other and filed the lesson where it files everything: families that quarrel beneath the crown stay beneath it. Serenity, as ever, was the strategy and the statement.
team.madonna.objective: Let the family argue beneath the crown, and collect what the quarrel spends.
team.madonna.definingEvent: Senna watches the sisters split the glasshouse, then posts the season's reference time anyway.
team.madonna.carryForward: The veterans' autumn will bring sentiment to the paddock; the king will bring none.

team.firenze.summary: Germi called the sisters' war the most beautiful thing the paddock had staged in years, and spent the season as its romantic chronicler, which rather summed up Firenze's year: third parties supplied the opera while the ice did the accounting. Elssler's FIRENZE 500 remained the craft formula's coldest instrument, the FIRENZE 99 V12's manners impeccable, the points banked in alpine silence. The drawing office's triumph was a fuel-usage evolution; its confession, that the wine-dark car had no answer for the family feud's theatre. Second to the crown, and watching, like everyone, the wrong fight.
team.firenze.objective: Keep the ice at the crown's throat while the paddock watches the sisters.
team.firenze.definingEvent: Germi names the family war the season's opera, and Elssler quietly banks the points it wastes.
team.firenze.carryForward: The veterans' autumn is Firenze weather, and the ice intends to make it a campaign.

team.millions.summary: The last quiet year before the bunting. With the paddock hypnotized by the glasshouse divorce, the chrome garage ran its most restrained campaign of the era, Alberti banking patrician arithmetic in the #6 while Jones supplied the volume at a slightly lower setting. The MILLIONS 189's DICK MD V10 was refined rather than replaced, the winter's spend going into a chassis-balance package that finally tamed the car's appetite for its front tyres. The garage's one loud moment was internal: the first whispers that next season would be the patrician's testimonial, and that everything would have to be worthy of it.
team.millions.objective: Run a quiet year, fix the front-tyre appetite, and ready the house for the Jubilee.
team.millions.definingEvent: The garage hears its own patrician will be honoured at every round of next season.
team.millions.carryForward: The bunting is ordered; the #6 must now justify sixteen rounds of it.

team.bestowal.summary: The marble house spent the family-war year as an interested spectator with a savings account. Every garage knew a new formula was being drawn somewhere, and Bestowal's winter went into readiness rather than the present: the BESTOWAL 167 received a careful evolution of its braking package and little else, while the benefactor's engineers hoarded budget for whatever came next. Blume banked correctness; Picos banked warmth and the odd brave Sunday. The VAPOR DN remained the grid's most envied straight-line instrument and its least consulted one, a cathedral organ played at weddings. The mood was patience with a balance sheet.
team.bestowal.objective: Hold position, hoard budget, and be first to the new formula's door.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: The benefactor quietly doubles the future-formula fund and halves the present one.
team.bestowal.carryForward: When the new dispensation is unveiled, the cathedral means to arrive with its sleeves already rolled.

team.blanche.summary: The white car thrives when the paddock argues, and the glasshouse war was a full season of argument. While the sisters consumed the press, Herbin went on doing the thing the whitecoats built him for: spotless stint after spotless stint, the BLANCHE 582 banking the points everyone else spilled. The DELTA 103 V10's thrift remained the midfield's reference. White added his usual brave afternoons and one podium near-miss the garage refused to call a disappointment. The whitecoats' only complaint was that nobody was watching, until they checked the standings, and discovered the establishment had been watching all along.
team.blanche.objective: Bank the points the quarrelling houses spill, and make the white car a standing result.
team.blanche.definingEvent: The establishment's mirrors fill with white for a third straight season, and stop being surprised.
team.blanche.carryForward: The veterans' autumn will honour old names; Herbin intends to make his the one nobody can file under old.

team.tyrant.summary: The calmest garage in the paddock spent the noisiest season of the era doing arithmetic. Hamano's crossover patience kept the TYRANT 548 at the front of the LEVEL B argument all year, the LIZZIE 24 V8's reliability a standing rebuke to garages with twice the budget. Gould amused himself between walls, and at Canada gave the island its annual late-braking sermon. The boot camp's internal war was a polite one: which of its two blades deserved the new front wing first. The sisters' quarrel, the crew noted dryly, was just intimidation practiced by richer people.
team.tyrant.objective: Stay the LEVEL B benchmark while the loud houses spend the season on each other.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Hamano out-banks both sister houses across the season's middle third.
team.tyrant.carryForward: The veterans' autumn suits the boot camp's cold virtues, and both blades are sharp.

team.losel.summary: The data door declared the season the most instructive campaign since the horsepower cut, and treated the sister-house war as a controlled experiment laid on for its benefit: same root, two philosophies, sixteen rounds of evidence. Pacheco's engineers read both telemetry streams like scripture while the #13 went on closing his own cold sums at the front of LEVEL B. The LOSEL 125's VAPOR DNPQ V8 ran its filed fuel curve to perfection. Dehehe's blue metronome ticked. The garage's conclusion, bound and archived, was blunt: fire wins rounds, frost wins seasons, and the family had spent a fortune proving it.
team.losel.objective: Study the family war as a controlled experiment, and bank the conclusion as speed.
team.losel.definingEvent: The archived verdict lands: frost beats fire across a season, and Losel writes it down.
team.losel.carryForward: The data men have the new formula's first principles already modelled, from someone else's evidence.

team.may.summary: Spring kept arriving on schedule. Turner steered the MAY 555 through the family-war year with the leaf-green garage's usual sunlight, mugging the distracted and waving at the grandstands, while the LORRY 32 V8 finished with metronomic loyalty. The season's genuine step was technical: a rear-suspension evolution, bought cheap and understood deeply, that gave the little car real traction out of slow corners for the first time. The crew chalked it up as proof that hope plus one good drawing equals progress. The sisters' quarrel barely registered in the garage. Blossom does not watch other gardens.
team.may.objective: Add one genuine technical step to the sunlight, and finish everything.
team.may.definingEvent: The new rear suspension turns the #15 into a slow-corner mugger for the first time.
team.may.carryForward: The veterans' autumn will be sentimental; May intends to be the young face in it.

team.bullets.summary: The planning year. While the sisters spent their fortune on each other, the backstreet garage spent almost nothing and learned everything: Ceara ran the featherweight BULLETS 560 on a shoestring and still haunted the edge of the big points, and every Sunday the lock-up crew grew more certain that one uncompromised car could do what the family's two compromised ones could not. The hand-fettled LIZZIE 24 V8 answered with stubborn reliability. Down in the lock-up, the ledger that would soon sell everything was quietly opened, and the first page was a list of what the garage owned.
team.bullets.objective: Spend nothing, learn everything, and prepare the one uncompromised car.
team.bullets.definingEvent: The lock-up ledger opens on a list of everything the garage owns.
team.bullets.carryForward: The new formula is coming, and the insurgent intends to meet it with a fully loaded gun.

team.dardan.summary: The oxblood house kept its peace for a second season, and the paddock began to find the silence interesting. Alfven's frozen consistency made the DARDAN 700 a fixture in the lower points, while Bellini, older, quieter, banked the veteran's craft he had spent a decade refusing to learn. The VAPOR DNPQ V8 minded its manners. There was one afternoon at Italy when the tifosi, bored of the sisters' opera, gave the old spear a cheer he had not heard in years, and something in the garage's posture changed. The feud with the sister garage stayed dormant. The edge stayed sharpened.
team.dardan.objective: Hold the peace, bank the craft, and let Italy remember the spear fondly.
team.dardan.definingEvent: The tifosi cheer Bellini for the first time in years, and the garage stands taller.
team.dardan.carryForward: The veterans' autumn will bring the oxblood car home to Italy, and the tifosi are waiting.

team.linden.summary: The old tree went on growing. Moreau steered the LINDEN LN198 through the family-war year with unhurried green patience, banking finishes while the loud houses spent themselves on opera. The LORRY 32 V8 finished everything, as it finishes everything. The garage's only drama was a cracked wishbone found in time, discussed for precisely one cup of tea. The paddock had begun to talk about the coming testimonial season for Alberti, and at the green garage the talk had a second subject: how many autumns the grid's own elder had left. Moreau declined to speculate and went back to work.
team.linden.objective: Bank the harvest, keep the root growing, and outlast another generation of noise.
team.linden.definingEvent: The paddock starts counting Moreau's autumns, and Moreau declines to be counted.
team.linden.carryForward: The veterans' autumn will need its elder, and the elder will be exactly where he always is.

team.minarae.summary: The forge's new racer spent the season proving the rebuild was load-bearing. Nono, patient now, turned the MINARAE 594 into a weekly nuisance at the front of LEVEL C, and the #21's points column stopped looking like a misprint. Miller, arms folded, allowed himself one sentence in public: the boy listens now. The SEGA SG1000 V8 gave everything it had, which the garage noted was no longer the limiting factor. The paddock that had laughed for a decade began checking its mirrors. The forge's fire was not lit yet, but everyone could see the smoke.
team.minarae.objective: Consolidate the rebuilt heir into the grid's most watched young racer.
team.minarae.definingEvent: Nono leads a LEVEL C train past two works cars and does not throw it at anything.
team.minarae.carryForward: The forge's fire is one good formula from catching, and the new dispensation is coming.

team.rigel.summary: The streak lived. Cotman and Chardin coaxed the old RIGEL 3000 through a third consecutive respectable campaign, the LORRY 32 V8 restored once more to rude health by men fluent in a car nobody else remembered. Cotman's finishing count reached a number he celebrated with exactly one glass of something modest; Chardin held his doors with undiminished courtesy. The museum's mechanics, emboldened, attempted their first genuinely new bracket in years rather than a faithful copy. It worked. Nobody outside the garage noticed, which made it, the lifers agreed, the perfect Rigel season: quiet, dignified, and secretly a little proud.
team.rigel.objective: Stretch the streak to three, and attempt one new part instead of a copy.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The first original bracket in years holds, and the museum allows itself a small glass.
team.rigel.carryForward: The new formula will test whether restorers can also be builders; the garage believes they can.

team.comet.summary: The streak stayed alive, which at Comet is the whole almanac. Tornio nursed the COMET 323 through the family-war year with his customary silence, and the black car reached the flag with a reliability richer houses no longer expected. The LIZZIE 24 V8 never once complained. The garage's romantics noted that the sisters' war proved their oldest sermon: one summer of glory outlasts a decade of squabbling. Hope remained a maintenance schedule. The mechanics rolled the car inside in November, checked the calendar for next season's grey afternoons, and circled two. The pan stayed cold. The faith did not.
team.comet.objective: Keep the streak breathing, and let the sisters' war prove the comet sermon.
team.comet.definingEvent: The black car logs its most reliable season since the comet year itself.
team.comet.carryForward: Two grey afternoons are circled for next season, and the garage means to be ready for both.

team.orchis.summary: The third glasshouse watched the family divorce with the interest of a poor relation reading a will. The Iris-Azalea split left Tegner's little operation as the last garage racing one flower under one roof. The ORCHIS 056's MISFIRE 50 V8 ran its second clean season, and the survival-first doctrine bought small, cool harvests on the grey afternoons the sisters were too busy to steal. The gardener-principal's ledger noted the year's true inheritance: when the loud houses quarrel, the quiet ones keep the scraps, and scraps, under glass, can be cultivated.
team.orchis.objective: Inherit the scraps the quarrelling sisters leave, and cultivate them under glass.
team.orchis.definingEvent: The last single-bloom garage in the paddock quietly banks its best harvest of the era.
team.orchis.carryForward: The flower has learned to live on leftovers, and the gardener considers that a breeding success.

team.zeroforce.summary: One more year of waiting, spent the way the floor spends everything: counting. Klinger drove the ZEROFORCE 231 with the patience of a man who knows the cavalry has been promised, and the old LORRY 32 V8, flattered by another gentle season, kept the chalk wall busier than the horsepower years ever managed. The marked date was one season away now, and the grey garage allowed itself a new ritual: at every round, one mechanic checked the prices in the customer-engine catalogue the paddock swore was coming. Gallows humour held. The lights stayed on. The date kept coming.
team.zeroforce.objective: Survive the last waiting year with the chalk wall busy and the lights on.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: The customer-engine catalogue becomes the garage's most-read document.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: The new formula arrives next season, and the floor intends to be first in the queue.

team.joke.summary: Dufay spent the family-war year doing what any sensible comedian does when the audience looks elsewhere: stealing the furniture. With every lens in the paddock aimed at the glasshouse divorce, the JOKE 777 mugged its way through the midfield, and the carnival's fake tyre panic evolved into a theatre of fake strategies that fooled exactly the people reading someone else's telemetry. The POND V8 ran thriftily, the ledger held in LEVEL B, and the #16's points column was the best of the Charter years. The sisters never noticed. That, Dufay said, was the whole point of being the punchline.
team.joke.objective: Rob the midfield blind while every lens in the paddock points at the sisters.
team.joke.definingEvent: The #16 banks its best Charter-era points column with nobody watching.
team.joke.carryForward: The veterans' autumn will be full of ceremony, and ceremony is the carnival's favourite stage.

team.lares.summary: The hearth-red house came back down to ordinary, and called it a good year. After the gentleman's summer, some regression was arithmetic rather than failure, and Arai's courteous patience still brought the LARES 92 home with the garage's whole religion. The RAM V12's winter fuel work held. The mantel gained a little less than the year before and lost nothing. The mood stayed warm: the lamp stays lit, the door stays open, and the paddock still nods when the hearth-red car crosses the line. The family across the racing world could quarrel; this one had supper to make.
team.lares.objective: Hold the gentleman's standard, accept ordinary arithmetic, and keep the lamp lit.
team.lares.definingEvent: The mantel's streak ends, and the garage opens the door for supper anyway.
team.lares.carryForward: The veterans' autumn is the hearth's natural season, and the kettle is already on.

team.feet.summary: The second tool arrived, slowly. The grafters spent the family-war year teaching the FEET 13 genuine speed to go with its sweat, and a winter-developed exhaust package on the YOUGEN V10 gave Rampal his first real straight-line argument in seasons. Old Boots still came home in the points by main force, but now the car occasionally arrived somewhere before the last lap, which the garage found disorienting. The pit lane's caps stayed tipped. The season's ledger read like the house creed: everything earned, nothing given, and for the first time in years a little of it earned quickly.
team.feet.objective: Add speed to sweat, and turn the drag home into something occasionally earlier.
team.feet.definingEvent: Rampal passes a works car on the straight, on merit, before the last stint.
team.feet.carryForward: The grafters have two tools now, and the new formula will be asked to respect both.

team.serga.summary: The paddock's conscience had its quietest year since the rescue, and the mountain man spent it profitably: welding, driving, and improving the SERGA 1000 one bracket at a time. The SC3000 F12 kept its unkillable manners, and the grey car's finishing column stayed respectable. The thousand small names faded a little in the sun and were repainted by hand over the winter, all of them. Sambena watched the sisters spend a fortune on a family argument and said, in his only interview, that some people will pay anything to avoid being grateful. The pit lane framed it.
team.serga.objective: Improve the hand-built car one bracket at a time, and keep every name legible.
team.serga.definingEvent: The thousand small names are repainted by hand, every one of them.
team.serga.carryForward: The grey car's creed, gratitude with a stopwatch, is about to become a national one elsewhere.

team.cool.summary: The deckchairs enjoyed the family-war year enormously, because nothing improves an afternoon like other people's drama. Delvaux chased the light through another gentle season, the COOL 05's CORSE V8 humming its sunset tune, and the garage banked its usual harvest of one festival and several fine afternoons. The sisters' quarrel was followed with interest and no opinion, the way one follows weather in another country. The only tension all year was a cooler malfunction at the height of summer, resolved with characteristic calm. The big board said what it liked. The light, as always, went gold right on schedule.
team.cool.objective: Watch the drama from the deckchairs, and bank the usual gentle harvest.
team.cool.definingEvent: The cooler fails in high summer, and the garage handles it without standing up.
team.cool.carryForward: The veterans' autumn will be emotional; Cool intends to enjoy it from a seated position.

team.moon.summary: The belief held. Moon circled through the family-war year on its usual distant ellipse, the MOON 292's RAM V12 lasting the distance ever more regularly, and Yepes kept waving the leaders through with the unkillable grin. The garage's low voices noted that the paddock had spent a season watching two rich houses fight while the floor quietly got closer. The telescope stayed up. The orbit, by the patient measurement of men who measure everything in seasons, tightened by one more degree. Every moon is on the rise, and the gap went down in chalk.
team.moon.objective: Tighten the orbit one more degree while the loud houses fight each other.
team.moon.definingEvent: The chalk measurement confirms the smallest gap to the pack in the garage's history.
team.moon.carryForward: The new formula is coming, and for the first time the telescope expects it to matter.

team.iris.summary: The split became open war, fought by the violet house on two fronts. Iris cut the IRIS 717 for Salgado's singing sweepers, the PRISM 90 V10 tuned to a razor's edge, and at Portugal the flame stood on his home pit wall and made the vow the tannoy repeated all season: one more crown before the fire cools. The white gloves gave the campaign everything; the sister house gave nothing, the season's second wound. The rehearsed photograph stayed in its folder. Monaco ended with violet and magenta parked nose-to-nose on the quay, then silence. The fire forgives no match.
team.iris.objective: Win the family war and the crown with the same campaign, and spend everything on both.
team.iris.definingEvent: Salgado's Portugal vow: one more crown before the fire cools, made loud enough for the sister house to hear.
team.iris.carryForward: The vow is now the garage's whole identity, and the veterans' autumn gives it one more stage.

team.azalea.summary: The frost bloomed. On her own side of the new wall, with the BLOOM 88 V8 behind her and the AZALEA 808's thin-air cooling package, christened the rooftop lung by a paddock that noticed its purpose, Larssen had the season of her life. Mexico's thin air was her cathedral, the banked sweep taken flat where the field lifted. The big board stopped pricing the #34 as a fixture and started calling it a bet. The glasshouse built its whole campaign around her, and the held breath of a decade finally opened. The sister house sent no congratulations. None were needed.
team.azalea.objective: Prove the glacial philosophy builds the truer jewel, with Mexico as the cathedral argument.
team.azalea.definingEvent: The rooftop lung takes Mexico's banked sweep flat, and the frost is crowned the family's bet.
team.azalea.carryForward: The bloom is open now, and the glasshouse must learn to defend what a decade built.
